reviews.review_description: First of all this game does not focus on combat, so think of it like a Red Matter game but with a focus on story and uncovering what's going on by exploring the environment. The graphics are great, the retro scifi aesthetic looks cool, the mechanics are good, the story is compelling, and overall it's very immersive. The game doesn't hold your hand, so don't expect a huge arrow pointing in the direction of where you need to go as you need to pay attention and read computer logs to unfold the story to know what you need to do next. The only downside of this game is that the combat is weak, but like in Red Matter 2 the combat is not the central part of the game, so it doesn't take much from the experience. The campaign is on the short size (4 to 6 hours), but it has a lot of extras to uncover that can stretch that playtime, and it even has multiple endings. For the price this game is a no-brainer.

I love this game, such a hidden Gem, it rocks. There are so many things to love about this game which was handcrafted by one person. 
The attention to detail was amazing. I loved all the retro aspects, from the Bladerunner style music on the admirals ship, to the retro games you have to play to unlock access.
The game has different aspects from cruising around in space to find things, to running around inside ships to find more things.
On top of that you have to deal with alien bugs that lend a sufficiently creepy quality to keep you on the edge of your seat, solve puzzles and finally,  hopefully, get to the end..........
Except there is more than one ending. So this is truly the game that keeps giving. There are even some easter eggs in there. 
For a solo developer this person sure has done a stellar job. 
I bought this after reading a mixed bag of reviews and wasn't sure how good it was going to be, but it was on sale, so I thought "what the hell, let's give it a go". Honestly this is one of my favourite games on meta quest that I've ever played. 
It took me about 10 hours to get to my first ending. But I could have ended it sooner with one of the other endings. 
My mistake was not saving as I went. To save time exploring the different endings make sure to save as you go. 
I would say the one drawback to this game, which isn't really a drawback but more food for thought for the developer. After about halfway through, the game  just got a lot easier. I thought the attacks would get more challenging and the puzzles harder, but I found the opposite. 
I would also love to be able to interact more with the surroundings. It would be cool if there were more things I could do with my spacecraft, like be able to zap the aliens, or have to use the grapper to move rocks out of the way to get to objects. 
Overall I just loved this game, I spent way to many hours hanging out in it. It was super fun, with lots of different environments, puzzles, a grand treasure hunt, creepy bugs. What more could one ask for............. MORE PLEASE:)

This is truly a masterpiece 👏👏👏✨️

This is my first purchase since I got the quest. And let me tell you this game delivers and more. 

The story got me hooked like a good book you can't put down, wanting to discover more, the more you go deeper down the rabbit hole. 

The visuals are stunning! One of the best I must say. I have a thing for the 80s from the ships interior, computers, overall design, etc. You really have the feeling you are there! 

The music and sound and truly amazing. It really plays into the atmosphere that you are in. At the right moment and cues. I was mesmerized.

Piloting your own ship felt like something truly spectacular visiting asteroids, planets, ships and discovering secrets. Having my own bedroom and bathroom was such a cozy feeling. 
I could spend hours just looking out a distant space or plants.

If you are into the 80's and a crave for Lovecraft vibe. Definitely get this one! 
My hats to the developers and the entire team for bringing such a gem.

Truly a must play / have!

This is genuinely one of the greatest games on quest right now. The story is fantastic and has depth and feeling to it, the graphics are beautiful, surpassing 90% of the triple A games on quest these days, and the visual design is superb and seems right out of Alien with the 80’s tech feel. 
AND ITS INCREDIBLY CHEAP

The only issue I have with the game right now is the length, it is only a few hours long if you focus on it (BUT, the games in beta still and mores being added so..)

Quantum Void stands out on Quest because it actually feels functional.

The registration system, access permissions, and real piloting make everything feel connected. You're not just watching — you're operating inside a system.

It would be great to see this expanded: finding and claiming new ships, renaming them, and taking on missions with real consequences.

You're on the right track. Build on what already works.

Great game and the best value for money so far. Its atmospheric(SPAAAACE), somewhat intuitive controlls and the story is low key but nice (i´m about half way through the game i think).
So from my point of view you can buy it even without a sale or rebate, it´s worth it.

Let me get this straight, the game is amazing. Peak story, peak visuals, amazing characters and amazing graphics. But the only problem with the game is that it’s short and can be beat in only a few hours if locked in and had unlimited battery. Still peak game though, keep up the good work!

reviews.review_title: cool game but I cant walk straight
reviews.review_description: I'm really enjoying the feel of the game. sorta calm and relaxing. it's beautiful and retro. I will love this game. BUT please fix the walk. when pushing forward to walk it drifts hard to the left. I have to walk with my joystick off to the right to counter a straight line walk. its like walking drunk, takes away some of the fun. please fix this:):) love it otherwise, so thank you very much.
This game is amazing, graphics are gorgeous and gameplay is incredible. Think Star Trek meets red dwarf without the comedy

reviews.review_title: An immersive and compelling VR adventure
reviews.review_description:
This is an amazing sci-fi game. There are moments of genuine tension and dread. It doesn't do an excessive amount of hand-holding, so you need to explore, look at maps,  and read information on computer screens and note pads to figure out what your next steps are. 

The look and feel of the fleet of ships you must explore, along with the sound effects you hear, seem to be a loving homage to the movie "Alien" and the "Halo" games. 

Navigating your way through space in your little maintenance shuttle makes one think of films like "Gravity" and "2001 : A Space Odyssey."

A truly spellbinding experience, lovingly crafted by a solo indy developer.
I'm going to trust that all of you are very much like ( yours truly) when it comes to gaming. We shall see.

This is great, I’m loving it so far. Except I’m STUCK on the first big boss, it’s SO HARD that mofo one hit kills me every time. I just had ONE thing to do and it got me lol!
The graphics are cool, the controls are great, I heard this is made by one person? That’s impressive if so! Either way, it’s a really cool game and I can’t wait to get past the baddie so I can explore more!

I appreciate the fiest 1hr 30min were good and the graphics were great, but after that i was jumping from planet to planet with no idea what IM supposed was searching for. I fell asleep with my headset on, it got so boring. At least make the menus more intuitive and the maps were really annoying. I pushed through trying to play the game coz i love scifi but it was so boring and directionless that i gave up.

Great graphics and was actually fun until I could not do a certain function.  So I threw a hissy fit, wrote a bad review and quit the game.  The developers, in the most kindest way possible, (not like the title) informed me of my failure to read the instructions.  
So I am extremely embarrassed and apologize for being so negative to a game that uses absolutely no AI for their awesome artwork.  Actually great game play.

reviews.review_title: Better than I could have imagined.
reviews.review_description:
I wrote a rather rough review of this when I first downloaded it, snd don't get me wrong, it was justified.

Game didn't start. Therefore, sucks.

Quite a bit of time passes by, I forgot that I had the game, and then rediscover it and reinstall in

I'm so glad that I did.

This game takes cues from Deadspace, Eve, Ghost Town (which you need to download for VR), Red Matter, Mass Effect, and very specific parts of Bioshock and rolls them into a phenomenal game of its own.

Now, it's not exactly like those other games - most heavily influenced by Deadspace - but it has their elements.

Not an over the top survival horror (though the environments are haunting), not pure puzzle based (though they are fun), not all space exploration (although THAT is a hoot), you can tell the environments, atmosphere, sounds, music, we're lovingly crafted (and i love the pet pictures!).

Do yourself a favor and buy this.

This game is beautiful, dark, and fills you with an uncomfortable dread. You are alone in this quiet section of space, trying to find your colleagues and wondering if this is even your universe. The game's narrative feels similar to that of iron lung; slowly drip-feeding you the story in bits and pieces that you the player have to find. The lighting of this game is like no other that I've seen on quest, and it really immerses you into the dark void. Sound design is fantastic as well, and I like the openness of the travel, letting you decide where to explore next. 




However, I do have a few gripes with the game as I do with all games I play. They don't ruin the experience, but I feel like these changes should be made to further immerse the player. The biggest one, the ship controls. You control the ship by grabbing the joysticks and using your controller's joysticks/triggers to maneuver around, which I find kind of immersion-breaking and a bit annoying. I wish I could physically manipulate the sticks with hand movement and use the triggers to throttle instead of turning. Another thing I wish could be implemented is a full player body. Having that extra part of you that you can look at makes you feel more like you're actually there, rather than just controlling a pair of floating hands. I also wish there were more weapon options in the game rather than the one multi-tool/gun you get. Speaking of I would like the multi-tool to be a bit more dynamic (like having a screwdriver function for panels, blowtorch for vents, shocker for electrical stuff) kind of like how tales from the galaxy's edge does it. It kind of just feels like a cheap toy. Last thing I would like to see is more interactables in the environments like random tools, papers, photos, and just more physics objects in general. Even just letting me open lockers/cabinets would make the experience more rewarding. Overall, this game is absolutely fantastic and worth trying out if you like narratives.

Just got corporate ending. That was like a game but a movie. I also wanted to lay on the floor after talking to the science team about the great attractor 😅😨 please make more games like this,it threw me off reading the data. Easily one of my favorite VR games to date,I plan on getting all endings. Can't wait for any dlcs future updates or new games by this team. a tiny bit more combat like a random infestation on your vessel while traveling locations in the star maps would make it a bit more fun imo but it's already perfect.
With red matter 2 the best space exploration/puzzle vr game ever, with darker atmospheres and more realistic colours

reviews.review_title: Needs a bit more Hand Holding
reviews.review_description: Been playing this game for a few hours now, so trying to give it a chance. I am finding the Stellar map extremely frustrating. I find it confusing and become bored with trying to navigate from one ship to another, once you get to the area you are trying to get there's the purple arrow  to follow, bit I feel more hand holding on the stellar map, would make the game less frustrating,  and wanting to give it up altogether, don't want to do that though,  because otherwise it's a good experience.  Hopefully this can be rectified.

reviews.review_title: A Quest 3 VR Dream Come True
reviews.review_description: Let’s time travel back to the early days of gaming, there was this Atari title called Star Raiders and you would fly through the galaxies to destinations and encounter situations like combat etc. however this was achieved with simple dots on your monitor that’s it! Most of it was imagination, now let’s teleport to now and that game is Quantum Void only instead of tiny white dots and imagination it’s fully realized in glorious VR. This game is just fantastic in every way a definite must have  as you jump into the pilots seat  of a fully rendered ship with crew cabin, sleeping quarters and even a bathroom! Travel the galaxies to destinations and dock with  ships to explore. The visuals feel incredibly immersive and atmospheric content are fantastic. Get this , buy this and live the Sci- Fi dream! Thank you devs for making this!

reviews.review_title: If you’re thinking about it, go for it.
reviews.review_description:
Easily one of the best VR games I’ve played in years. For such a low price, the game truly delivers on every aspect promised. Want to get up and explore your ship whilst flying? Want to solve puzzles and unravel the mystery of what is really going on behind the scenes? Want to experience a feeling of uneasiness everywhere you go? This is the game for you.

 It’s astounding to realize that this was made by such a small team. There’s live action video/cutscenes, a fully in-depth computer system, and so much content. This was truly one of the greatest VR titles of recent years for me, and I can’t wait to see what else this developer brings!

They're very proud of the fact they don't use AI, I don't really care, but it's absolutely stunning, it's immersive, complex and huge! Genuinely amazing if you want a puzzle space game set in an an "alien" style world

It's not bad, but it's not great either. I think th dev needs to get off his anti AI high horse and spend more time working on pacing and content. It's honestly a slog and nothing made me feel compelled to keep going. I'd have refunded it, but I kept hoping it would have more to offer.

Love this game and controlling your ship
 and exploring is amazing. All credit to the developers who have done a truly exceptional job with the graphics. Not being that into shooters just the occasional aliens to deal with suits me fine. Great job guys and I look forward to ehat you do next. Love the dogs by the way.
The gameplay is alright... but the story is where its at

I'm super disappointed. I would have given this a great review as I was really enjoying the game. Got to the frozen moon and the game just crashed. I'm stuck in the pilot seat looking at empty ice, the segments c and d have disappeared. I can't fly the plane and can't even get out the seat. I've tried restarting it but it's still stuck. So now I would have to start all over again which I don't think I want to do. So my experience with this game ends there unfortunately.

Great story, great graphics, fun to play. The first thing I did when I undocked my ship was get lost in space, and the best part was that the game allowed me to do it. The game is still in early access, so this might change in the future, but I would like to see more interaction with the environment and maybe some collectibles to find besides posters. With multiple endings to be found, the game is well over 8 hours to play. Yet another game proving the indie devs are where the greatest games are coming from.

Superb graphics for Quest. Gameplay is fun but not necessarily too simple to get everything without a little bit of thought.  Would definitely recommend without hesitation. If you like RedMatter but wish it had just a little bit of enemies to battle, this fills that void.  On the default level, at least, enemies do not occur to often nor to difficult to defeat. I really like the balance.

reviews.review_title: Wow! But any chance we get a co-op game?
reviews.review_description: First I thought, not another indie title that is plain boring with meh graphics but WOW this is an AAA title. I just wished there was some sort of campaign co-op game to play with friends. This is what is missing with most games to play story "with" friends not against them. The whole ship would be a great start for a new co-op game or DLC maybe. There is Interstellar Strike very good co-op concept but underwhelming graphics and content. Maybe you guys can join together?

An authentic game with no AI or generative models used in its creation all done by a single developer with a particular and rare vibe I've only experienced in The Backrooms, but in space. 

I just have one request: please implement virtual joystick ship control option so I don't have to use the thumbsticks. It's a great option not just for immersion, but for a large number of people who experience notorious stick drift. an example of a game that has this feature is "Metacity Patrol".
There are parts of the game where you get into a deadened and unable to continue. Not a very user friendly programming.

Not long got this game to try out and not too far in, just heading back to segment A after A, B and now back to A and saved from there, just updated it so will see what its like with the latest update later 

So far it seems a decent concept and works well  etc and great well done for one guy making this 👏 I just hope theres more to come in enemies, upgrades etc, and I get the jist of the travel forwards and backwards to the ship segments but I'm really not liking that bit, its soooooo annoying and tedious, having to keep guessing where to fast jump too, I get the idea of it but its just fast jump, then fast jump and then keep fast jumping some more to try and get where you want to be, so yeah I totally agree with another review on here that, that part is just pure tedious and there is no fun in having to do that for me anyway, this is why I csn only give it 3 stars

But so far decent game apart from that well done dev guy 👍, just hoping it gets better 🤞I'll find out i guess and if so, it'll get an extra star ⭐️
The game has definitly exceeded my expectations. Have enjoyed every minute of it. Would Highly recommend that  anyone who likes sci-fi games get this one !!

Note to developer: theres a glitch where if you stand up while in a going through a vent you can see a lot of the map

I really adore this game. Outstanding graphics but really fun addictive gameplay that feels rewarding and fulfilling . With a good story to boot.
It is a very good game. It has hours of gameplay and the graphics are the best I've seen in a game yet it is also really engaging with the story

reviews.review_title: Good game marred by playability issues
reviews.review_description:
Overall, it manages a nice balance of exploration and puzzle-solving, though the puzzles are mostly find-the-password-in-another-area.  Maneuvering your ship at a location is straightforward and fun.  However the UI for the network of locations makes it difficult to identify the remaining un-visited locations once you've visited a bunch. The "super-jump" (fast travel) capability is confusing — it's not at all clear when or how you can use it.   There's a large number of NPCs, and so far all the clues about them I've found appear to be flavoring — if you're supposed to deduce something about them, the needle got lost in the haystack.

The exposition fairy is probably necessary, but I'd get much more sense of achievement, if it waited for me to consult it, so I could try to figure out things on my own, first.
The game keeps track of passwords found and other pieces of information, but there's no way to recall them away from certain terminals, which really limits the usability. If Horizon allowed you to use a note-taking app or webapp while playing, it would be far easier to keep track of things. Taking screenshots gets you some of that, but you need the ability to write out text also.

The difficulty is not hard, until the battle against the giant spider.  There, you have to figure out what the game wants you to do while trying to fight off the spider, which is really aggravating — you'll probably die lots of time before you figure things out, which I don't find fun at all.

Everything about this game is incredibly solid! There's the very tactile way you interface with the computer systems, the pacing that mixes things up with fun new mechanics, the incredible sound design, and yes the graphics which includes a lot of dynamic lighting, some key physics objects, and some gorgeous vistas.

There's some light puzzling, some light combat, some good humour, a whole lot of mystery solving, and multiple endings!

It's not a long game, i clocked it at about 6 hours speeding through, but it doesn't waste your time, and at this price is very much worth it.

I had the privilege of alpha testing this jewel.  Being early access it’s alreadt better than 99% percent of released titles.

Graphically is absolutely phenomenal.  Lighting, textures, the feel of everything is amazing.

Compelling non linear story with several possible endings.

Considering this is a solo developer title it’s an unbelievable achievement.  Could perfectly be a 30€ title, but no, Le Dev is humble and kind enough to sell it at 10.99.

GET IT RIGHT NOW!
The moving is rigged I moved forward moved me backwards side to side never forward it is mixed up on all of them pls fix this still five stars but fix this

Just started, but the atmosphere is awesome. Some of, if not the best standalone graphics I've seen on quest 3. Everything is super sharp and detailed. Makes me wonder what other devs have been doing...
I love how the menus are in game computers you have to interact with. Cant wait to play more.

I wish ship could be flown with "physical" controls

Quantum Void has some good things going for it. I can see why people are excited. I especially enjoy the computer/keyboard interactions. But, there are a few things that could use some work…

It would be great to have a “worm hole” for a more direct fast travel between ships/segments. At least until there is more to do on these flight paths. Having to travel from point to point to point feels tedious. 

More interactions with the environment would be very appreciated. Doesn’t have to be a lot either. But to be able to open at least one locker/cabinet/desk drawer every now and then. 

The combat feels like an afterthought. As do the enemies. I really hope this gets the most attention during development. It really breaks the immersion and puts me in what feels like an old-school arcade light gun game. I suggest adding some “weight” or impact to the gun attachment or the projectiles. Having less enemies but make them harder with a couple more attack/damage animations. 

Despite my gripes. I’m glad I bought this game. A lot of hard work was put into this. For now, I’m going to step away from the void. Good luck! I’m excited to see where this goes.

I love the atmosphere of this game. It’s very Alien (retro-future) in style, and full of little funny elements. I love how you pilot the ship with the stick, and not with a virtual stick that doesn't exist.
And I like to solve puzzles and not be stressed / scared.

I’m giving this game five stars because it’s a great game especially if you like red matter and red matter two which were fantastic. this game  it was so cheap to buy. I could not believe how good it is. ￼
 ￼ if you like adventure, especially in space is great ￼game I don’t know how many hours I spent in it but it just keeps on going. I keep coming back. Love the game  ￼￼
Great looking game but its bugged in a mission and I freezes the ship so I can’t move to the waypoint.

developer_response.body:
Hello, sorry this happened in your play through.
Can you give more details about the mission/moment of the game this happens? (Either here, or on the social media of your preference)
That way I can fix it. Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
I can't believe this has been made by just one dev with a little bit of help from his friends, it's just so good, the quality is up there with the best, Red Matter 1+2 included. It's currently a work in progress but the quality is outstanding and continues to be improved. The gameplay is fun and sometimes makes you jump out of your skin, there's plenty to explore and things to figure out, it's more of find something at "A" which you have to use at "B" etc with combat , some of which is truly nerve shredding! 
A good game to get lost in the quantum void!

This game is out of bounds good.  For the price, it's a no brainer.  The art work is insane and the immersion is beyond incredible.  I am a space nerd and this is what is vr is all about. I feel so lucky to be alive at a time to experience this stuff.
really fun! a mix of Alien Isolation, Elite Dangerous and The Room with a good sense of humor mixed in. 5 stars for sure.

Best Adventure Game of 2025

This is a need to have game. 
Adventure, puzzle and action space game.
Spectaculair graphics.

100 pro developers might not be able to make a game as good as this. That’s cool one guy did it with some help from some friends, but this game stands on its own. It has that thing you can’t buy. Not sure how to describe it. Love? Devotion? Attention to detail?

I had the opportunity to beta test the early parts of the game and I must say, it felt I was playing a PCVR title.

The graphics and effects are among my top 3 for stand alone vr and it is truly impressive you have things like volumetric and real time shadows which is unheard on a mobile platform, if you want to show off to your PC gamer friends, hand them this game. 

The controls are very intuitive and immersive,most of all action are phycallised in other words, you must reach out and do instead of pressing buttons, and the story is very interesting, I can't wait to play the game and discover all the things I missed and possible experience all endings.

Judging from my test gameplay I would say the game will be around 8-12 hours long to finish depending how your pace is and not counting the other endings.

I am more impressed about the dev, which was really cool when taking criticism, and adopting beta testers like myself suggestions and updating the game so quickly.

Simple amazing and please support devs like him, they only bring joy and great experiences for our platform.

A great story of cosmic horror with awesome environments, some good puzzles, and a splash of combat. The emphasis is really on exploration with focus on the unfolding story. There are also some great set pieces with tense moments. Maneuvering in your spacecraft and traveling to different exploration points was very well done. A minor complaint would be the amount of backtracking involved for some core updates which I feel could have been streamlined. Otherwise a solid game that is a must play for any fan of the genre.

I love this game. After spending lots of hours I can’t seem to get passed the large plant. This is ruining my experience. If only there was a run or move faster option in this game or not let the giant plant move as fast! Thank you for your replly on the running mode to defeat the large plant 👍 I’ve updated to 5 starts. I’m stuck on getting past the large flying monster whilst trying to collect all 3 backboxes i usually can only the one but after 100s of tries cant seem to pass this level.

developer_response.body:
Hi ! 
Thank you for the kind message :) 
this part is about timing and staying out of the view of the beast as much as possible. It tends to stay on one side of the asteroids, so that you can stay on the other side. 
If you struggle too much, keep in mind that there is a "easy" mode in the game options. This mode will slow down the monster and make this part easier to pass.

reviews.review_description:
I can’t believe how well developed this game is and if it didn’t mention it was early access then I would not have noticed.

It’s highly immersive, I have played nothing else for weeks, just this!  Taking my little ship on adventure to adventure.  I won’t say much more as the game unfolds as you play and there are a lot of surprises waiting for you.

Just get it and play, wonderful work developers, not played a game that hooked me like this in years!

reviews.review_title: Unprepared for how OUTSTANDING this is..
reviews.review_description:
Quantum Void - I was unprepared for how good this is

I just started this game and I’m pretty blown away. I saw GamertagVR, BMFVR, and a few other creators cover the game about a month ago, but I didn’t give it any thought. I already had Alien: Rogue Incursion and Into Black installed, and I’ve seen enough gamers describe ‘peak’ Quest graphics by evoking Red Matter 2, so I just assumed that the game looked good and that was it. At best, i figured,  it’s a great looking space puzzle game or whatever. 

And while Quantum Void is absolutely one of the best looking games on Quest 3 (easily on par with Batman: Arkham Shadow, Red Matter 2, and Ghost Town), the graphics really aren’t what made me post this.  

The game design elements - everything from user interface, to art direction, to sound design, are OUTSTANDING.  Full stop.  I’ve played a LOT of VR games, and Quantum Void takes the best design elements of games that work, and  weaves them into a polished package that makes it difficult to believe that this was done by a small team. No AI.  This game is punching FAR above its weight.  I literally haven’t posted ANYTHING about VR in almost two years, but I had to come out to bring some more attention.

This isn’t a review. It’s more of rambling collection of reactions that I’ve experienced through my limited time with the game. The framework is basically that you’re some kind of maintenance worker clearing debris from larger space ships. Something freaky happens, and you’re sent - Event Horizon style - to some psychedelic uncharted sector of the universe with limited radio contact, and only the barest plan on how to get back. The non-linear experience alternates between ship and on-foot exploration, but it’s all creepy and sublimely executed. 

A few quick thoughts: 

Art Design - straight up Ridley Scott Alien. Futuristic ships with retro tech. Analog and Commodore interfaces in small cozy ships of brushed metal and dulled reflections. Dangling trinkets and chunky controls. Think a trucker’s cabin, but a little roomier.  Everything sits at the right height. You never feel too tall or short, and it when you’re in a space mission, it *feels* right proportionally. You really inhabit the space, and it’s amazing.  

I won’t list all of the games that have tried this to varying success on Quest, but the closest games I can think of that execute this well are Vox Machinae, Iron Rebellion, and the menu part of Star Trek: Bridge Crew.   To a lesser degree, Revria. 

Ambient light and shadows from outside actually create real atmosphere on physical items around you. Again, there’s an incredible sense of presence that i haven’t felt in most VR games in a long time.  It’s all pretty immersive. 

Design and interface - The dev made some incredibly smart choices here. Lock to ladders and climb or descend.  None of the dreaded, “oh boy, I have to climb down a VR ladder and I hope I’m in the right spot” nonsense.  Asgard’s Wrath did this well, too. I’m glad to see this developer taking notes. 

Lock into chairs and other interfaces. Press a button to magnet lock into a chair is common, but resting your hands on any keyboard, then using the analog on the quest controllers to choose a pre-selected story phrase as the animation mimics typing is excellent.  You’re not hen-pecking through keys. You’re always perfectly positioned and the immersion is never broken. 

Flying your ship feels like a simpler but smoother version of Space Docker VR. You magnet lock to the stick controls, and there’s almost ZERO learning curve to moving up and down, forward and backward in space. Sure, there are no physics, but they aren’t missed. It’s easy, you feel awesome, and you stay in the experience. 

Atmosphere is something I’ve mentioned a few times, but there’s something design perfect when all of those elements  work in concert. 

For example, early on I’m in the other verse and I’m communicating with my bosses back home. I’m headed to a nearby debris field to investigate what happened to the ships that came before me. I dodge some larger asteroids and initiate a short hyper jump to the destination.  The communication tells me that my previously inactive systems in the lower part of my ship are now operational.  

There’s no ‘cutscene’ or break in the progress, I choose to disconnect from the keyboard (by just lifting my hands), I hit Y to disengage from the chair, I turn around in my ship, climb downstairs, and in my sleep bunker, my computer is operational again. I walk up to the terminal, place my hands on the keyboard, and by flicking the analog on the quest controller, I have a several menu options but the GAME shows my hand ‘typing’ and moving with the controller, giving light rumbles to indicate feedback. 

Together, the experience FEELS organic and real.  I stop interfacing (when I feel like it), lift my hands, climb back up the ladder, back into my seat, place my hands on the sticks, magnet lock in, and start navigating my ship toward the destination.  All seamless.  

Seems trivial, but it’s incredible.  

So far, I’m LOVING this experience.  It’s literally the type of game that you want to show people. I can’t speak on how the game holds up in the back half, but the fundamentals are SUPER strong no matter where the storytelling or action goes. It’s already a top tier experience , and one that you probably shouldn’t miss out on trying because 11.99 seems crazy for what feels like a game worth far more.

reviews.review_title: A must buy for the sc-fi fan!
reviews.review_description:
I was very kindly granted access to test the game. I was not expecting to feel so connected to it. The feel of this game gives an 80s vibe which I'm all for. Voice acting is excellent, some very well-done parts at the beginning. I was grinning ear to ear. I don't want to spoil anything, so if you like a good puzzle esque story driven space exploration with some great humour and genuine wow moments in VR then do yourself a favour and grab this now. I had about 2 hours game play time but plan to go in and finish the game shortly following early release. 

To say this is a single dev it's outstanding what they have achieved here.

I have over 270 games in my collection in vr games, this is by far the best, considering how it was made. One guy??? WOW!!! I am truly amazed at the artistry and attention to detail. I have played this game 2 times start to finish and I still keep finding things I missed the first time. The story line is phenomenal 👌.  Can't wait to see what is next for this game and look forward to what is next for this Dev. Congrats on a top tier game. Absolutely amazing. Can't wait to see what you have in store for us next.

I'm re-reviewing (or previewing?) this now that I've thoroughly completed the game and experienced two endings (including the true ending). 

In terms of sheer ambition, this is a GOTY contender for me but with a few asterisks after it. 

The graphics and sound are top notch, but what really makes this game special is the story and the lengths the developer(s) go to convey the sheer scale of this Lovecraftian, multidimensional, mind-bending romp that spans many ships and systems but all within the constraints of the Quest platform and what a small team can do. It's rare that a game can make me feel small like this or make me exclaim "wow, that's so freaking neat!" over and over again. What's astounding is that it's a $15 Indie giving me these reactions, not some AAA game with a budget hundreds of times larger. 

This game is infinitely worth it and you should pick it up immediately if you like games that prioritize immersion, exploration, problem-solving and story well above action and instant gratification (the game is slow-paced but does offer some big payoffs and cinematic thrills). 

All that being said, I do have some small nitpicks and issues with the game but after reading the roadmap, a lot of those nitpicks are being addressed by the developer(s) during the Early Access period so I look forward to replaying this game from beginning to end in a year or so.

Sorry to lowball the score (meanwhile other folks are being too kind due to the story of a lone developer).  This is a good game for what it is but it's not better than Batman Arkham Shadow or something, fam.  

Anyhow, I will change my lowball score when there is a better explanation for piloting the ship.  I felt like I had to figure it out on my own.  For a while, I only flew backwards at a diagonal angle or something and it was making me quit in frustration.  I figured it out but that was definitely a sore spot. The short explanation that pops up for ship controls states to use sticks but only shows the right stick highlighted and the left grip highlighted. I was looking mainly at that image.  So I was only trying to use the right stick.  As far as signifiers go, that controller image needs updated with proper signifier design for what you are trying to convey.  Show highlights to all things being referenced.  It totally threw me off.  I should not feel dumb and want to quit the game due to a bad signifier.  It needs reworked.  Trust me. I will leave this review at low stars until it is fixed. Because, I promise, to someone just picking your game up, that can be frustratingly confusing.  That's bad design. Good design is invisible, unappreciated.  Bad design immediately sticks out.  It's a quick fix of a graphic. That's all. 

I will check the game out again if it ever leaves early access.  The developer has built up a lot of good will with the story of being a lone developer and I believe this is reflected in the reviews.  I am not trying to be a hater.  It's cool but it's not some mind blowing game.  It's not going to be considered for game of the year.  No, the graphics are not better than the Red Matter games.  Stop overhyping this.  It's a good 4/5 first effort from a developer with a lot of potential.  It's an impressive first effort.  That's what it is.  Bam.  I will change the star number later.  But, this is early access and deserves an honest critique. 

But, for early access, aside from inadequately explaining piloting controls (Was it just me???), I did not see any VR jank.  It is a fairly locked down game to achieve that lack of jank (you're not going to be interacting with a lot outside of mechanical steps to advance the story progression) but, again, minimal jank. 

Still needs some polish.  But this is a promising start for the developer.  

Hey, developer, I hope the hands are early access.  They honestly look weird.  

Doubtful, but it would be great to get Crossbuy support upon full release.  That would do a lot for that developer cred.

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you so much for the critique! 
I take notes and will improve on all of this before leaving early access in a few months. This is helpful.

reviews.review_description: I bought this on a whim and I’m blown away. Not only is it one of the best VR games I’ve ever played, it’s one of the best games period. It’s a rare example of a game that gets everything right. The gameplay, the story, the art direction, the level design, it’s all equally amazing and makes for an incredibly compelling game. The atmosphere feels inspired by classic sci-fi movies like Alien in the best way too. It evokes nostalgia for those classics without feeling like a knock off of them. The feeling of exploration it gives you is executed beautifully as well. The first time I warped to another location and then docked with another ship I could get out and fully explore just felt magical. The overall production value is also mind blowing. I’m incredibly impressed that an indie dev was able to achieve such a beautiful looking, polished feeling project. It feel triple A in every sense and you never get the feeling that it compromises anywhere. I love everything about this game, if anyone is on the fence about getting it I highly recommend pulling the trigger. I don’t think you’ll be disappointed. Thanks to the amazing dev team for putting this out into the world. I’ve been waiting for a good space exploration game to come to Quest and this delivers in spades.

At first glance, I expected Quantum Void to be another short-lived addition to my sci-fi game library. Instead, within an hour, it had completely exceeded my expectations. The visuals are striking, the scale is grand, and the story draws you in with surprising depth. What’s truly astonishing is that all of this was achieved by a single developer. Quantum Void stands out not just as an impressive indie project, but as a remarkable sci-fi experience that deserves attention and praise.

This game was the biggest surprise In VR for me. The. graphics are Red Matter 2 quality, the ships are huge and incredible to explore, the ship controls are great and it's so fun to explore this universe hyper jumping across space. It has an 80's space film vive, and even though it is still in early access I never experienced any bugs. Incredible work and congrats to the developers. I finished the game (multiple endings) and also forgot to mention the game was 11 bucks. Best 11 bucks I've ever spent!!!

reviews.review_title: A great atmospheric solo space adventure
reviews.review_description: I have been I. The beta for this game for the last 3 months. It is staggering what one dev has done. The atmosphere. Implementation of VR mechanics puts other much bigger game devs to shame. It’s come a long way in the 3 months I’ve been involved and the dev will keep on going. Worth every penny and more. If you like space adventures this is for you. Super Jump in and enjoy.

Great game! I wish more devs put this much love into the right details that make a game shine. And shine this game does in spades. 

So many times I found myself grinning at cool moments that I didn’t anticipate. The story is great and makes you want to keep going to figure out what happened.

Everything about this game is excellent. The triple A game devs need to hire  guys as a consultant on how to not make a  bad game with infinite money and resources.since we constantly get Meh games for $70 when something like this is worth way more as far as polish ,immersive game play, and  story. You nailed  it thank you!

I'm thrilled by such attention to detail and mastery.
The atmosphere, the graphics, the sounds, the story—everything is there to make this game a hit.
The first shock comes from the visual quality; it's a feast for the eyes!
Then, very quickly, we find ourselves caught up in the atmosphere; the immersion is total.
The story is immediately addictive.
We pilot our tugboat with ease and fun between debris and stellar objects, moving on foot, scrutinizing each terminal in search of clues or life forms.
Replayability is ensured by multiple endings and an open-ended storyline.
Quantum Void impresses with the precision of its design: detailed graphics, immersive sound, and solid narrative writing.
This independent French project could well become a benchmark for spatial narrative VR, combining immersion, mystery, and depth.
Congratulations and thank you, dear developer!

Very disappointed after all the great reviews. For me it's just extremely dull. A lot of going back and forth in pretty, but repetitive locations. Graphics are nice but down to the lavish lighting and shaders that can be turned up to 10 as there isn't anything going on - like animated characters. Maybe I am missing something but my 2 hours is almost up and I am refunding as it's just got nothing to it. Loved the Red Matter games and thought this might be similar but there is no plot to get you involved. Just no hook to make me want to go round in circles looking for clues. Gets an extra star for the sole developer.

I just started playing this amazing game, but I already love what was made. I am big fan of space games, and haven’t seen many for VR yet, especially so original as this title. So far I love every minute of it, great graphics when exploring places inside the ships, a space itself graphics could have been a little better, but I accepted it as the game itself is great. I love controls for the ship and in general, I haven’t seen such an uncomplicated and intuitive control yet in this types of games anywhere. If you are a fan of that types of games I would recommend it. Thank you for the great game.

This game is extraordinary. It was developed by just one person, yet technically it’s on par with much larger titles made for Meta Quest 3. But it’s not just about the graphics — the adventure itself is really well-crafted, structured, and engaging. Please, if you enjoy the genre, you can’t miss the chance to try it and support such a talented indie developer.

reviews.review_title: No Teleport or Motion Sickness Options
reviews.review_description:
Contrary to people who will say those options take away from being immersive and all you need to do is blah blah blah... Not everyone can adapt to free motion. It does seem to look great, however I couldn't get far enough before I felt sick. There are no teleport or motion sickness reducing options (ie vignette) other than reducing or stopping head shaking. It seemed ok for a bit but headache and naseau set in pretty quickly. Unfortunately not for everyone. 

From what I was able to play before having to request a refund, it looked really interesting and I wish I could have dived further in.  I understand it's a small team, possibly 1 person that hand drew this game (it states in the first screens that AI wasn't used), so it is definitely visually impressive for what it is.

developer_response.body:
Hi!
Thank you for the feedback, and apologies for the motion sickness.
A teleportation option will be added in upcoming updates.

It is indeed a solo developer project, so work time had to be managed to make sure to reach the early access step with enough content. From now, more accessibility options will be added fairly soon.

reviews.review_description:
Everything, from the art style, the graphics, the audio design and music, from beginning to end, one big masterpiece made by a solo developer!

Get this man a big team led by him, or at least get this man a bigger budget to work on! The fact he’s pulled this off on what he’s working on, is without understatement, in one word, incredible.


Props to Tactical Nounours for creating this piece of art.

I look forward and will be keeping my eye on this and future projects involving this skilled developer.

Disclaimer: this review was made before the official meta store launch day, in beta testing. I did not get paid to say any of this. My opinion is my own. 

-Patuber

reviews.review_title: WOWOW! This game is WAY unpriced !
reviews.review_description:
First off, I do have to make known there is something off when keying in your name to create profile. When I move joystick to the right, the highlight of the keys would move down, when I move down, it would either go
left or right. And sometimes later, when I’m moving around, I would feel this slight “pull” to the right or left so think these two discrepancies are somehow connected. 

Otherwise, just WOW! Did this game inject me with hallucinogens cuz this experience is a trip and a half! From the ship undocking and take off from the dock, maneuvering the ship over giant asteroid fields and ginormous spikes in a space cavern, the visuals in this game is just absolutely an experience to behold. Highly recommend!

Such an awesome game!  Non-linear exploration is a relatively untapped concept for VR and this game executes upon it very well.  From scene to scene its a fully seamless and immersive experience, no loading screens in sight even as you transition from on foot to piloting your ship. Controls well, plays well, and is quite gorgeous visually, especially regarding the way the lighting from the stars floods the ships interiors.  It had me hooked the entire time and there are some legitimately cool moments I'd never seen from VR before. But, if its lots of action you're seeking this one might not be for you, as it's slower paced with emphasis on systematically exploring the solar system to unravel the mysterious events occurring and there can be a lot of backtracking involved to collect everything. Despite being early access, its well polished and loaded with high production values, even offering different endings depending on the path you take.  Well worth a play through and one of my favorites this year.
Honestly, the game looks terrible, and it actually gives me motion sickness. I completely regret buying this.

reviews.review_title: Why all the excitement over this game?
reviews.review_description:
Unfortunately this is going to sound a bit brutal, but it's my honest opinion of this game so far.

Yes the graphics are nice, although I think the graphics on Red Matter 2 are nicer, but that's just personal opinion. However what sets these two games apart is the storyline and gameplay.

So far I'm about 2/3 though and I'm finding it totally boring and repetitive. Basically all the gameplay consists of is  jumping from one area of space to another to find missing spaceships with no one on board, and wandering around a maze of corridoors and rooms, which all look virtually the same, and interacting with computer terminals, with the occasional attack from wierd space creatures thrown in.

There is almost zero interaction with ones surroundings or other people. The only thing one interacts with is a computer terminal.

I've reached the part where I'm trying to recover black boxes from some asteroids but am unable to complete due to a completely pointless space creature attacking my ship.

This for me is the point where this game goes in the bin as I cannot see the point in trying to get through this section of the game just to go find another empty vessel and aimlessly wander around yet another maze to interact with yet another computer terminal, and maybe shoot at some more rock creatures.

The game is nice to look at, but if you want immersive and interactive gameplay then it's one to avoid.

I'm glad I only paid £9 for it.

Ok great job on this and early still so definatlly recommend and for the price it’s cheap but follow link to get discount and u r pretty much stealing it from devs on this one , it’s genius and not close to being finished. I’m mor of a story game person so I do hope the difficulty pans out right caus it’s close to genotype and it’s difficulty level to me wasnt too accurate but maybe I can see entire game fingers crossed . Also only 4 stars cause im profoundly deaf and no subtitles yet. it does hav objectives in what u r supposed to do but I’m hearing others talk n background which I do assume it’s my characters voice and whoever is guiding me, but no clue caus subs not added in all no option n menu as of yet anyway wil change if gets updated w those for sure but great so far 😃

- one of the best looking games on Quest. Especially lighting and reflections are cool. Try to shoot lightbulb to swing it. Space itself is gorgeous, enormous and fun to explore. 

- interesting non-linear story with multiple endings. The ending from executive office made me laugh, totally rewarding.

- already extremely polished even though it is early access. I experienced no bugs (I mean it, NO BUGS). Just same lags during the space jumps, but thats no issue.

- collectables and funny secrets hidden all over the space. If you dont know why funny, you will understand, when you find THAT room XD

- quite unique story and gameplay. Sometimes it can be little bit repetitive, but few minutes later the game surprises you with new twist and this keeps you going. Well done!

- metroid kind of approach, you unlock more = you can access more in the game. You want to go back to previously explored locations to find out if there is any secret to discover.

- one developer did work of whole development team on his own. I am truly amazed. I still dont understand how this is possible.

- not many things in the environments are interactive at this moment, but this will maybe change with later updates. You can still shoot them and they react based on the gravity of the environment.

- the fighting is little bit arcade, but as it is not main part of the game, it is not an issue. It adds nice spiciness to the game.

- gameplay for completionist is about 5 hours. The completion will reward you with new secrets and game endings. Totally worth it!

I cant express enough how cool this game is. It is already a gem in VR games and for its price it is a no-brainer!

The best looking game I think I've played so far on my quest 3, the fact this was made with all hand made art work no Ai and I believe one guy, is outstanding. I'm about two hours in and I love the story, the atmosphere and the mechanics. For money this is a bargain!! On a side note I currently make synthwave music and have scored some horror films and would love to work with you on any future projects my artist name is DeltaCity look us up if interested and you read this or reach out on insta at deltacitymusic. Amazing job on this game 👏👏👏👏

I want to start out saying this game is fun and can't wait for future updates. That said, it definitely needs some improvement. For example,  when you get your weapon and shoot those mineral bugs , the reload is awkward. It seems much better if you reload from the bottom and not the top of the weapon. Virtually,  every VR game reloads from the bottom or flicking your wrist. Secondly , an option for combat practice would be good , instead of replaying entire episodes to get used to the weapon.  More puzzles and mysteries to solve per episode would be fantastic.  Its definitely worth buying,  and i honestly can't wait to see the final product.

Update 2025-10-5:

The micro stutters are gone!  I didn't really think about the micro stutters until they were gone but now that the game runs as smooth as silk I can see what a difference it makes.

The world feels extremely solid and the immersion is just incredible.

The game was already beautiful and pushing the Quest hard before the update. I still find myself hitting the dangling lamps from time to time just to watch the realtime lighting and shadows.

The only thing that breaks the immersion now are the yellow hand icons that appear on the back of your gloves when you can interact with objects. I really wish that there was a way to switch those off and just have subtle haptic feedback.

The only glitch I've found is that the game over message usually doesn't appear in front of you if you play standing and use natural turning. You have to either recenter or turn around and look for it. Initially I thought the screen just went blank when you died. For some strange reason most Quest developers never test with natural turning which seems odd since it's a wireless headset, but it's just the way it is. However compared to the usual bug filled messes on the store this game is squeaky clean and is now approaching "flawless" quality.

Update:

I'm now totally used to the graphics in the flight sequences and I've become hooked on the exploration.

To me it's not a horror game, but it's very unsettling and a bit nerve wracking at times and there are some real "Wow!" moments. I just got to another one tonight that literally made me say "wow!" out loud. 😁

This is a fantastic game, one of the best.  I'm raising my score from a four to a solid five stars.

It's not flawless, (what game is?), but it's still absolutely wonderful.

Original Review:

Nearly everyone is going to be talking about the graphics rendering technology, but what's likely going to hook me on this game is the story and the art direction. This is a mysterious and fascinating place. I'm not going to discuss the plot and I recommend everyone go into this game knowing only what's been shown in the trailer.

I've been playing video games for over 40 years so the 80's aesthetics are very nostalgic for me.

The UI and general gameplay are mostly really slick. The way they resolved the awkwardness of using a keyboard in VR is very clever and best solution I've seen so far.

The only issues I have with the VR design are that you can't interact with objects that you should be able to like locker doors, coffee mugs, and clipboards, and objects that you can move react in a "muted" manner, as if you're as weak as a kitten. It's a bit strange.

The audio is fantastic! Very detailed ambient sound, convincing sound effects, skilled use of reverb, etc. Top notch work from the developers. Snippets of music are well composed, perfectly fit the style and add to the drama, but it's mostly music free which keeps the game feeling ominous.

The graphics are mostly excellent. The lighting is among the best I've seen in my decade of playing VR. There are some jaw dropping visual effects. Everyone's naturally going to compare this to Red Matter. I'll have to compare the two games back to back, but based on my memories of Red Matter, this game eclipses it in some ways.

I say the graphics are "mostly excellent" because it was a bit of a let down when I first got to fly a ship.  The view out the window looks somewhat flat, like it might not be rendered stereoscopically. At times it's like watching a flat movie. To boost performance Star Wars Squadrons on PlayStation VR1 didn't render the environment outside your ship in stereoscopic 3D, and maybe that's what they're doing with this game to get it to run on the Quest 2. (Thankfully Quantum Void doesn't suffer from the reprojection ghosting that plagued Star Wars Squadrons.)

I eventually got used to the flat environments in Star Wars Squadrons and I'll get used to them here. It's possible that increasing the contrast on the objects in space to bring the shadows up to the level that's shown in the Steam version in the trailer would give it more depth. If it isn't stereoscopic, (I can't tell for sure yet), then possibly this could be a Quest 3 update in the future.

However the beautiful and eerily realistic shadows and sun glare on the windows is one of the best lighting effects I've seen in a video game and goes a long way towards making up for the flat look.

The bottom line is that everyone who has any interest in a space exploration game like this shouldn't hesitate to purchase it. And even if you have no interest in this sort of game you should give it a try, you might discover another genre that you enjoy.

And hey Meta!  You really need to get a trailer up on your YouTube channel and promote the hell out of this game. It'll make a lot of Quest owners very, very happy and sell headsets and make more Quest owners. This needs a wider audience than just the people who happen to stumble upon it while browsing YouTube.  I'm so glad I was lucky enough to discover it.

not very clear on how to navigate your way from planet to space craft
very frustrating could do with a help guide
i wont listn to beardo benjo again
trying to navigate to next space craft very anoying
would love to get my money back 
it realy needs a better help. guide
i am removing this game

Every so often there is a little know game that catches my surprise.
Quantum void is one of these in a space based adventure game that mixes exploration, combat and some light puzzles.

There is a hint of metroid about it where you retread old ground as you unlock "programs" that allow you to access previously inaccessible areas.

There in an interesting story to drive you to the end where I believe there are multiple endings to find.
Ill be having fun figuring out the unlock criteria for each ending.

Whilst the game is still an Early Access release honestly Im more than happy with the state of the game now.

So im curious what else is coming outside of a little polish.

Im even more impressed after finding out it was mostly a solo passion project.....I would never have guessed based on the game itself.
A indie darling
